title: Ridge Runners
desc: Danian operatives are moving through Ravanaugh Ridge. You need proof — and maybe a scan.
chainDesc: A conversation about an old friend draws you into Maxxor's deepest wound.

steps.text:
Ravanaugh Ridge. Wind cuts through the narrow passes like a blade. The terrain here is all vertical — sheer cliff faces, rope bridges, ledges barely wide enough to stand on. Perfect for creatures that don't want to be found.

You pick your way along a ridge trail, scanner sweeping. For the first hour — nothing. OverWorld patrols in the distance. A few wild creatures. Normal readings.

Then your scanner picks up something. Three bio-signatures clustered in a ravine below, partially masked by the mineral density of the rock. But the masking isn't natural — it's deliberate. Someone is running signal dampeners.

You find a vantage point and look down. Three Danians. Not soldiers — they're too careful, too quiet. One is sketching patrol routes on a tablet. Another is testing something in a vial — your scanner flags it as organic, parasitic, and very familiar.

The third spots you.

options.label: Hold your ground and get a scan before they reach you
Run immediately — the proof on your scanner is enough

steps.text:
You hold your nerve. The Danian is fast — already scaling the cliff face toward you — but you've got maybe fifteen seconds before he closes the distance.

You point your scanner down at the one with the vial. Full sweep. Your device locks on and starts rendering — carapace structure, internal anatomy, the parasitic compound in the vial registering as a separate bio-signature layered on top.

Ten seconds. The climber is halfway up.

The scan completes. You've got a full Danian bioscan and a chemical analysis of whatever's in that vial.

Five seconds. You can see his mandibles now.

You run. Behind you, chittering — the alarm call. All three are coming. But you know the ridge trails better than they do, and Danians aren't built for vertical sprints on narrow rock. You hit a rope bridge, cross it, and cut the far anchor with a sharp rock.

The bridge swings down. The lead Danian stops at the edge of the gap and watches you go. He doesn't look worried. He looks like he's memorizing your face.

options.label: Get back to Kiru City with the scan data

steps.text:
You don't wait. The moment the Danian's eyes lock on you, you're already moving — scrambling back along the ridge trail, boots slipping on loose stone.

Behind you, the chittering alarm call echoes off the canyon walls. They're coming, but you've got a head start and the high ground. You duck through a narrow crevice that a Danian's broad carapace can't fit through, then double back along a parallel trail.

Your scanner still has the readings — the three signatures, the parasitic compound, the signal dampeners. It's not a full bioscan, but it's enough to prove Maxxor's suspicion. Danians with parasite samples, running covert operations in OverWorld border territory. That's not diplomacy.

You hit the main trail and don't stop until the ridge is behind you.

options.label: Get back to Kiru City with the scan data
